title: Let cloners hide the template link
question: Can a cloned project remove the 'Get the free template' link from its viewer?
rationale: Add a per-project _project.json flag, hideTemplateLink, that removes the 'Get the free template' button from the top bar. Default stays shown, so a published explorer still points people back to the tool (d46); someone using the skill in their own project can opt out so their explorer doesn't advertise it. Reuses the existing _project.json personalization channel (name, icon), so it's one small field, not a new mechanism.

tradeoffs.note: cloners turn it off in their own projects; the maker's published explorer keeps it on
one optional field on _project.json, read where name and icon already are
still on by default, so the promo survives unless a project explicitly opts out
every explorer links back, maximum reach
a cloner's own explorer advertises someone else's repo with no way to remove it

options.label: Make the whole top bar configurable

tradeoffs.note: users could set their own wordmark and link target, not just hide it
more schema and rendering for a need no one has yet; hiding covers the actual ask
